stories.title: Export Request as cURL
stories.spec:
Acceptance criteria:
- Right-clicking a request item in the Sidebar shows a 'Copy as cURL' context menu entry.
- Clicking it calls the Go binding ExportRequestAsCurl(requestID) and writes the result to the clipboard via navigator.clipboard.writeText.
- The generated command is a valid, single-line curl invocation (backslash-newline continuation is optional but preferred for readability).
- Method: emitted as -X <METHOD> (omit -X for GET if desired, but emitting it is fine).
- URL: includes all enabled query params appended as ?key=value pairs.
- Headers: each enabled header emitted as -H 'Key: Value'.
- Body:
  - json/raw → -d '<body>'
  - urlencoded → --data-urlencode for each key=value pair
  - form-data → --form 'key=value' for each enabled pair
  - none → no body flag
- Auth:
  - bearer → -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'
  - basic → -u 'username:password'
  - apikey addTo=header → -H 'KeyName: KeyValue'
  - apikey addTo=query → appended to URL
  - none → no auth flag
- A toast/snackbar confirms 'Copied to clipboard'.

Technical approach:
- New Go file: curl.go (package main).
- ExportRequestAsCurl(id string) (string, error): loads request via db.GetRequest, builds the curl string, returns it.
- Uses encoding/json to decode headers/params/body KV arrays (same kvRow struct as httpclient).
- No new DB schema changes.

Files likely affected:
- curl.go (new)
- frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx (add context menu entry)
- frontend/src/wailsjs/go/ (regenerated bindings)

stories.title: Import from cURL — dialog
stories.spec:
Acceptance criteria:
- A sidebar header button (or collection context menu entry 'Import from cURL…') opens the ImportCurlDialog modal.
- The modal contains:
  - A <textarea> labelled 'Paste curl command'.
  - A <select> listing all collections (populated from collectionsStore) for the user to choose where to add the request.
  - An 'Import' button and a 'Cancel' button.
- On Import, calls Go binding ImportFromCurl(collectionID, curlCommand).
- On success: modal closes, sidebar refreshes the target collection, the new request is selected in the editor.
- On error (parse failure, file reference, empty command): an inline error message is shown in the dialog; modal stays open.
- If the user cancels, no request is created.
- The name of the created request defaults to '<METHOD> <host+path>' (e.g. 'POST api.example.com/v1/users').

Technical approach:
- New component: frontend/src/components/ImportCurlDialog.tsx.
- Controlled by a boolean state in Sidebar (or a Zustand flag).
- Calls window.go.main.App.ImportFromCurl after validation.
- On success dispatches refreshRequests action from collectionsStore / requestsStore.

Files likely affected:
- frontend/src/components/ImportCurlDialog.tsx (new)
- frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx (trigger button + mount dialog)
- frontend/src/store/collectionsStore.ts or requestsStore.ts (refresh after import)
- frontend/src/wailsjs/go/ (regenerated bindings)

stories.title: Go backend — curl serializer & parser
stories.spec:
Acceptance criteria:
- ExportRequestAsCurl(id string) (string, error) is bound on App and callable from the frontend.
- ImportFromCurl(collectionID string, curlCommand string) (db.Request, error) is bound on App and callable from the frontend.
- Parser correctly handles:
  - Bare positional URL (last non-flag token)
  - -X / --request for method
  - -H / --header for headers (split on first ': ')
  - -d / --data / --data-raw / --data-binary for raw/json body
  - --data-urlencode for urlencoded key=value pairs
  - --form / -F for form-data key=value pairs
  - -u / --user for basic auth (splits on first ':')
  - Single-quoted, double-quoted, and unquoted token values
  - Backslash-newline line continuations (strips them before parsing)
- body_type inferred from Content-Type header: application/json → json, application/x-www-form-urlencoded → urlencoded, multipart/form-data → form-data, else raw.
- If command contains --data @file or --form field=@path, return a descriptive error (file references not supported).
- Empty or whitespace-only curlCommand returns an error.
- ImportFromCurl inserts the request via db.InsertRequestInFolder + db.UpdateRequest, same as existing import flow.
- Unit tests for both functions in curl_test.go.

Files likely affected:
- curl.go (new — ExportRequestAsCurl, ImportFromCurl, internal helpers)
- curl_test.go (new — unit tests)
- app.go (no changes needed; methods live in curl.go but in package main so they are bound automatically via the App receiver)
